name: Molinos y castillo de Consuegra — circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Consuegra are primarily characterized by the historic Calderico Hill, home to the iconic windmills and castle. The terrain often involves moderate ascents and descents, providing varied running experiences. The region's landscape is open, offering expansive views, particularly around the hill. These routes generally follow well-defined paths, suitable for exploring the area's cultural landmarks. Best jogging routes around Consuegra The most popular jogging route is Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 37 minutes to complete. This route offers direct access to the historic castle and the famous windmills. Another top favourite among local runners is Molinos y castillo de Consuegra — circular, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This circular route provides panoramic views of the Consuegra castle and windmills. Local runners also love the Consuegra Castle – Calderico Hill loop from Consuegra, a 4.6 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through the historic Calderico Hill, often completed in about 47 minutes. Jogging around Consuegra is defined by historic castles, iconic windmills, and open hill paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all routes currently rated as moderate. The routes in Consuegra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 180 runners have used komoot to explore Consuegra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find in the Consuegra area?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Consuegra, all rated as moderate, offering a variety of experiences for runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Consuegra?
A: Yes, many routes in Consuegra are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210132637/molinos-y-castillo-de-consuegra-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos y castillo de Consuegra — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 3.4-mile option that provides panoramic views of the castle and windmills. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24531656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle – Calderico Hill loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 miles long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Consuegra?
A: The jogging routes in Consuegra are primarily characterized by the historic Calderico Hill, meaning you can expect moderate ascents and descents. The landscape is generally open, offering expansive views, especially around the hill, and routes often follow well-defined paths.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while running in Consuegra?
A: Many running routes offer stunning views of the iconic windmills and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle&lt;/a&gt;, both located on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021483917/consuegra-castle-and-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle and Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering direct access to these historical sites.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Consuegra?
A: All routes currently listed in Consuegra are rated as moderate, meaning they involve some elevation changes and are suitable for those with a reasonable level of fitness. There are no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' for absolute beginners in this selection, but the shorter moderate routes could be a good starting point.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Consuegra?
A: The running routes in Consuegra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. Runners often praise the unique historical scenery, particularly the windmills and castle, and the varied terrain that keeps runs interesting.

Q: Are the running routes in Consuegra suitable for families?
A: While all routes are rated moderate, families with older children who are accustomed to some elevation and longer distances might enjoy the trails. The historical landmarks like the castle and windmills provide interesting points of interest along the way. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderico Hill – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt; for a scenic family outing.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trails in Consuegra?
A: Consuegra is a town with various parking options, especially near the base of Calderico Hill where many routes begin. You can typically find street parking or designated parking areas within the town, making access to the trails convenient.

Q: Can I access the running routes in Consuegra using public transport?
A: Consuegra is accessible by bus from larger cities like Toledo. Once in Consuegra, many of the running routes, particularly those starting from the town center or the base of Calderico Hill, are within walking distance from public transport stops.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Consuegra?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times for jogging in Consuegra, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are typically mild but can be chilly, making it suitable for running with appropriate layers.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for experienced runners in Consuegra?
A: Yes, for those looking for a longer run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40310386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 5.1 miles (8.2 km). While still rated moderate, its length provides a more extended workout compared to some of the shorter loops.
tips: It is here where you can find, among others, the Town Hall (1670) of this municipality as well as the Municipal Archaeological Museum.

Cerro Calderico rises in the middle of the La Mancha plain, being visible for many kms. away and giving Consuegra (Toledo) its own stamp of identity. As the most relevant heritage elements we find the Castle and the Windmills that extend along its top following its longitudinal profile. Its views of the mills and castle, worth seeing.

It is a limestone elevation, arranged in a N-S direction forming a monocline, about 100 meters. altitude above the surrounding plain and 1,500 meters long, located next to the town of Consuegra and about 67 kms. away to the south of the city of Toledo.
